title: Fewest stable segments, lexicographically-smallest cuts (two-pointer reach + min-segment DP)
source: Partition into the fewest contiguous segments with max-min<=K, returning the lexicographically-smallest end indices among all minimum partitions. Intended solution: O(n) two-pointer reach[] + right-to-left min-segment DP + earliest-legal-cut search; an O(n^2) feasibility recompute times out. Grader ground truth: independent brute force that enumerates all 2^(n-1) partitions on small inputs.
